summary: Orphaned SUIUSDT position with no stop loss, no take profit, no grid orders, 5.16% to liquidation
symptom: SUIUSDT Buy 330 @ $1.06359 (15x, value $351) has no open orders, no bot attribution (bot_count=0, bot_id=null). Bot 8580f06d is in error state. Margin monitor tried to add margin but got 'Amount below minimum'. Liquidation price $1.0025 is 5.16% away from mark price $1.057.
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge.json positions section: bot_count_for_symbol=0, bot_id=null, bot_attribution=none, stop_loss=null, take_profit=null
runtime_snapshot_bridge.json open_orders: symbols={} (no open orders)
bots.json: bot 8580f06d status=error, last_error='CLOSE_FAILED profit_lock_fast: ws_timeout_after_send'
margin_monitor_state.json: SUIUSDT last_add_error='Amount below minimum'
runner.log 09:14:11 CLOSE_FAILED event, 09:14:39+ ERROR state persisting every 5min
account equity $32.54, position value $350.99, effective leverage 10.8x, available balance $5.74
suspected_root_cause: Bot attempted profit_lock_fast close via WebSocket, got timeout. Bot transitioned to error state. Error state excludes bot from position attribution loop. Position is now unmanaged on exchange with no protection orders.
notes_for_fixer: URGENT: This position is live on exchange with real money. Priority 1: verify if position should be closed or if bot should be recovered to manage it. Check if error-state bots should still claim their positions for attribution. Check if auto-recovery from CLOSE_FAILED is possible. The margin monitor is trying but the amount is below exchange minimum.

summary: Public WebSocket completely stale — no live market data for 17+ minutes
symptom: All public stream topics (ticker, orderbook, kline, public_trade) last received data 1052+ seconds ago. desired_ticker_symbols=[] and desired_orderbook_symbols=[] — stream has zero active subscriptions. Private WS is connected but position/order caches are marginally stale (5.5s > 5.0s max).
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge.json meta.stream_health.topic_last_message_at: ticker=1773648877 (17min ago), orderbook=1773648877, kline=1773648876, public_trade=1773648876
desired_ticker_symbols: [], desired_orderbook_symbols: [], desired_kline_symbols_by_interval: {}
private_cache.position: fresh=false, age=5.5s, max=5.0s
private_cache.order: fresh=false, age=5.3s, max=5.0s
suspected_root_cause: When no bots are in 'running' state, the stream service may unsubscribe from all public topics. With SUIUSDT bot in error and all others stopped, desired_symbols becomes empty. This means the system has no live prices even though there is an open position on exchange.
notes_for_fixer: Check bybit_stream_service.py — does it only subscribe to symbols for 'running' bots? If a bot is in error state but has an open position, the stream should still subscribe to that symbol's data. Also check if the stream reconnection logic handles the case where subscriptions drop to zero.

summary: SUIUSDT bot 8580f06d stuck in ERROR state with no auto-recovery for 17+ minutes
symptom: Bot entered ERROR at 09:14:11 after CLOSE_FAILED profit_lock_fast ws_timeout_after_send. Guardian detected cycle stuck (302s, then 335s, 381s, 423s, 464s, 503s, 542s). Guardian applied heals (reconciliation, stuck_flag clearing) but could not recover the bot from error state. Bot continues to log error every 5 minutes with no recovery path.
evidence: runner.log 09:14:11: CLOSE_FAILED bot_id=8580f06d close_reason=profit_lock_fast response=ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 08:59-09:14: Guardian cycle_health CRITICAL warnings escalating from 302s to 542s
guardian_healing_log.json: 10 entries for 8580f06d — reconciliation, stuck_flag, cycle_health events
runner.log 09:14:39, 09:19:44, 09:24:45, 09:29:48: ERROR state logged every 5 min with no recovery
suspected_root_cause: CLOSE_FAILED is a terminal error state that guardian cannot auto-recover from. The bot tried to close a position for profit_lock_fast via WebSocket, got a timeout, and the error handler set the bot to error status. There is no retry/recovery path for this specific error type.
notes_for_fixer: Check grid_bot_service.py error handling for CLOSE_FAILED. Should ws_timeout_after_send trigger a verification of whether the close actually succeeded on exchange? If the position is still open, the bot should attempt recovery. Check if there is a manual recovery path (e.g., setting status back to running after verifying exchange state).

summary: Effective leverage 10.8x with only $5.74 available, margin monitor blocked by exchange minimum
symptom: Account equity $32.54, SUIUSDT position value $351, effective leverage 10.8x. Available balance only $5.74. Margin monitor attempted to add $0.1004 margin but exchange returned 'Amount below minimum'. Liq distance 5.16%.
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge.json summary: equity=32.54, available=5.74, effective_leverage=10.8
margin_monitor_state.json: SUIUSDT last_add_usdt=0.1004, last_add_error='Amount below minimum'
positions section: pct_to_liq=5.16, liq_price=1.0025, mark_price=1.057
suspected_root_cause: The margin monitor correctly identified the low liq distance (5.17% <= 8.00%) and attempted FIRST_RUN margin add, but the calculated amount ($0.10) is below Bybit's minimum margin transfer. With only $5.74 available and the position consuming most of the equity, there is limited ability to add margin.
notes_for_fixer: Check margin_monitor_service.py — the minimum add amount should meet exchange minimums. Also check if there is a fallback action when margin add fails (e.g., partial close to reduce exposure). With 5.16% to liq, this position needs either more margin or partial closure.

summary: Dashboard snapshot timeouts — 153 bots_runtime timeouts in current app.log
symptom: app.log shows 153 'Dashboard snapshot timeout for bots_runtime after 1.0s' warnings. Multiple per minute. Also occasional 'summary' timeouts at 1.5s threshold. Dashboard may be showing stale data to the operator.
evidence: app.log grep count: 153 timeout warnings
suspected_root_cause: The bots_runtime snapshot generation is exceeding the 1.0s timeout threshold. Likely caused by expensive computation in bot_status_service.py building runtime payloads, especially when readiness evaluation triggers API calls (kline data, indicator computation). The stale public WS data may be forcing REST fallbacks.
notes_for_fixer: Check bot_status_service.py and runtime_snapshot_bridge_service.py for what happens during bots_runtime snapshot generation. Consider increasing timeout or caching expensive computations. The operator sees stale dashboard data during these timeouts.

summary: Excessive entry gate log spam — UNIUSDT every 5s, HYPEUSDT every 3s
symptom: UNIUSDT 'ENTRY_GATE blocked (short): Support 0.04% away @ 3.9340 (strength=10)' logged every ~5 seconds for 20+ minutes. HYPEUSDT blocked messages every ~3 seconds. These dominate both runner.log and app.log, making it difficult to spot real operational issues.
evidence: runner.log: UNIUSDT blocked messages from 09:23 through 09:30+ at ~5s intervals
app.log: HYPEUSDT + UNIUSDT blocked messages combined at ~3s intervals
Both logs are >90% entry gate messages
suspected_root_cause: Stopped bots are still being evaluated for readiness on every fast tick cycle. The entry gate correctly blocks them, but the log message is emitted every cycle rather than being rate-limited or suppressed for stopped bots.
notes_for_fixer: Consider rate-limiting entry gate log messages for stopped bots (e.g., log once per minute instead of every cycle). Check entry_gate_service.py or entry_readiness_service.py for where these messages are emitted. The readiness evaluation itself is fine; it's just the logging frequency.

summary: Entry gate boundary: setup quality 42.0 rejected as below threshold 42.0
symptom: Runner log shows repeated 'ENTRY_GATE blocked (short): Setup quality 42.0 below 42.0' for TRIAUSDT. Strict less-than comparison means a score exactly equal to the minimum threshold is rejected.
evidence: runner.log: 20+ entries showing 42.0 below 42.0
suspected_root_cause: entry_gate_service.py uses < instead of <= for setup quality threshold comparison
notes_for_fixer: Check entry_gate_service.py for the setup quality comparison. Determine if the strict < is intentional (conservative) or should be <=. The message text 'below 42.0' is slightly misleading when the value equals 42.0.

summary: Systemic guardian healing: capital_starved and silent_bot flags across 7+ bots, 10+ symbols
symptom: Guardian healing log shows 50+ stuck_flag clearances for _capital_starved_block_opening_orders and _small_capital_block_opening_orders across multiple bots. Also silent_bot clearances and position_health resets. Pattern has persisted throughout the day.
evidence: guardian_healing_log.json: 80+ entries across PIPPINUSDT, TIAUSDT, BNBUSDT, UNIUSDT, SOLUSDT, LINKUSDT, BANUSDT, SUIUSDT, CFXUSDT, ZECUSDT
Repeated every 120-170 seconds per bot
suspected_root_cause: With account equity at $32.54 and most capital committed to the SUIUSDT position, remaining bots cannot afford to open positions. The capital_starved flag gets set, then guardian clears it after timeout, then the next cycle sets it again. This is expected behavior given the low available balance ($5.74), but the frequency suggests the guardian healing threshold may be too aggressive.
notes_for_fixer: This is likely not a bug but a consequence of the SUIUSDT position consuming most available capital. However, the healing loop frequency could be reduced to avoid log noise. Consider: (1) longer guardian timeout for capital_starved flags when available balance is provably insufficient, (2) suppressing repeated identical heals.

summary: Margin monitor not retrying after initial 'Amount below minimum' failure
symptom: Margin monitor detected pct_to_liq 5.17% <= 8.00% threshold and attempted FIRST_RUN add of $0.1004. Exchange rejected with 'Amount below minimum'. Monitor has not retried in 25+ minutes despite liq distance worsening to 5.04%. last_add_ts unchanged since 09:10.
evidence: margin_monitor_state.json: last_add_ts=1773649833.56 (09:10), last_add_error='Amount below minimum', last_seen_pct_to_liq=5.07
Current pct_to_liq=5.04 (worsening)
No new margin add attempts in runner.log since 09:10
suspected_root_cause: Margin monitor may have a cooldown or only attempt FIRST_RUN once per session. After failure, it does not retry with a larger amount or trigger alternative actions (partial close). The 'Amount below minimum' error means $0.10 is below Bybit's minimum margin transfer.
notes_for_fixer: Check margin_monitor_service.py for retry logic after add failure. The monitor should either: (a) retry with a larger amount meeting exchange minimum, (b) trigger partial position close to reduce exposure, or (c) escalate to operator. Current behavior is silent failure.

summary: Guardian service appears idle — no heals or warnings since 09:24
symptom: Guardian last logged error_persist warning at 09:24:44. Since then (12+ min), no guardian heals, no cycle_health warnings, no reconciliation events. Bot 8580f06d continues to log ERROR every 5min but Guardian is not responding.
evidence: runner.log: last GUARDIAN entry at 09:24:44 (error_persist)
runner.log: ERROR state logged at 09:29:48 and 09:35:06 with no guardian response
guardian_healing_log.json: last entry at 08:14:00 (cycle_health)
suspected_root_cause: Guardian may only monitor bots in 'running' state. Once a bot transitions to 'error', it may be excluded from the guardian's active monitoring set. This means error-state bots with open positions receive no watchdog oversight.
notes_for_fixer: Check guardian_service.py — does it filter bots by status? Error-state bots with open exchange positions should still receive guardian monitoring. At minimum, guardian should escalate unrecovered error states with open positions.

summary: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: error state excluded from LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES — position orphaned
symptom: SUIUSDT Buy 330 has bot_count=0 and bot_attribution=none despite bot 8580f06d existing for SUIUSDT
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge_service.py:24 — LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES = {running, paused, recovering, flash_crash_paused} — does NOT include 'error'
runtime_snapshot_bridge_service.py:741 — bot_lookup only includes bots with status in LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES
Bot 8580f06d status='error' → excluded from lookup → bot_count_for_symbol=0
Also missing from set: 'stop_cleanup_pending', 'risk_stopped'
suspected_root_cause: LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES in runtime_snapshot_bridge_service.py (line 24) does not include 'error' status. When a bot enters error state, it loses position attribution even though it may still own a position on exchange. This breaks margin monitoring, stop loss placement, and all bot-aware position management.
notes_for_fixer: Add 'error' (and possibly 'stop_cleanup_pending', 'risk_stopped') to LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES at line 24 of services/runtime_snapshot_bridge_service.py. This will restore position attribution for error-state bots. Also check if other services use similar status filters that exclude error state. IMPORTANT: This is a real-money production fix affecting position safety.

summary: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: stream subscriptions only include 'running' bots — error/stopped bots with positions get no market data
symptom: Public WS completely stale for 25+ min. desired_ticker_symbols=[]. No live market data.
evidence: runner.py:446-454 — symbols = {bot.symbol for bot in bots if bot.status == 'running'}. Only running bots included.
runner.py:463 — stream_service.set_symbol_subscriptions(symbols) called with empty list when no running bots
With SUIUSDT bot in error and all others stopped, symbols=[] → all public topics unsubscribed
runtime_snapshot_bridge.json: desired_ticker_symbols=[], desired_orderbook_symbols=[], ticker stale 1510s+
suspected_root_cause: risk_check_all_bots() in runner.py only gathers symbols from bots with status=='running'. When the only active bot (SUIUSDT) entered error state, the subscription set became empty. set_symbol_subscriptions([]) unsubscribed all public topics. Even though there is an open position on exchange, the system has no live market data for it.
notes_for_fixer: In runner.py:446-454, extend the symbol set to include symbols from error-state bots (or any bot with status in a 'has_open_position' set). Also consider adding symbols for which there are actual open positions on exchange, regardless of bot state. The current logic: only running → no data when only bot is in error with open position.

summary: MON-001 RECURRING: Two new orphaned positions — SUIUSDT Buy 70 + PUMPFUNUSDT Buy 10300, both bots in error, no SL/TP
symptom: After operator closed the first orphaned SUIUSDT position, two new positions appeared. SUIUSDT Buy 70 @ $1.0534 (15x, liq 7.51%) and PUMPFUNUSDT Buy 10300 @ $0.0020854 (8x, liq 15.53%). Both positions show bot_id=null, bot_attribution=none. Both bots (8580f06d SUIUSDT, 6055cb3b PUMPFUNUSDT) are in error state. No stop loss on either position. Runner still reports 'No running bots.'
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge positions: 2 positions, both bot_id=null, bot_attribution=none, stop_loss=null
bots.json: 8580f06d status=error, 6055cb3b status=error
runner.log 09:45:50: 'Ignored stale control-state save for bot 6055cb3b: incoming status=running version=35, current status=error version=35'
runner.log 09:43-09:44: Auto-Pilot entry hold cleared events
runner.log 09:45:52: 'Runner active: No running bots. Waiting...'
suspected_root_cause: Same as MON-001: LIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES excludes error. Also: bot 6055cb3b attempted to transition from error→running but was blocked by stale control version. Auto-Pilot may have triggered position entry but the bot couldn't maintain running state.
notes_for_fixer: URGENT RECURRENCE. Same root cause as MON-001. Additionally, investigate the stale control-state save blocking for 6055cb3b — why is status=running being rejected when current is error? The control_version match (both 35) suggests a race condition in the error→running transition.

summary: Systemic ws_timeout_after_send — 2 bots hit same CLOSE_FAILED within 1 hour
symptom: PUMPFUNUSDT bot 6055cb3b hit ws_timeout_after_send twice at 09:45:38 (partial_tp_fast) and 09:45:42 (profit_lock_fast), entering error state. Same error hit SUIUSDT 8580f06d at 09:14:11. Both bots now in error with unmanaged positions. This is a systemic WS reliability issue.
evidence: runner.log 09:45:38: CLOSE_FAILED bot_id=6055cb3b close_reason=partial_tp_fast response=ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 09:45:42: CLOSE_FAILED bot_id=6055cb3b close_reason=profit_lock_fast response=ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 09:14:11: CLOSE_FAILED bot_id=8580f06d close_reason=profit_lock_fast response=ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 09:41:20: Connection to remote host was lost (private WS disconnect)
Two different bots, two different symbols, same ws_timeout_after_send error within 1 hour
suspected_root_cause: WebSocket reliability issue causing timeouts on close order sends. The private WS connection may be degraded or intermittently losing connectivity. The 09:41:20 disconnection event confirms WS instability. The close operations are sent via WS but the confirmation never arrives within the timeout window. This is aggravated by the CLOSE_FAILED → error state transition which is terminal and has no recovery.
notes_for_fixer: The H1 audit fix (ws_timeout → ambiguous) would prevent bots from entering error state on this failure. But the underlying WS reliability issue should also be investigated: why are close order sends timing out? Check bybit_client.py WS send logic, timeout thresholds, and whether the send is actually getting to Bybit. Also check if the WS connection was actually dead when the timeout happened.

summary: PUMPFUNUSDT hit CLOSE_FAILED partial_tp_fast: ws_timeout_after_send — H1 fix DOES NOT PREVENT this failure
symptom: At 09:45:38, PUMPFUNUSDT bot 6055cb entered CLOSE_FAILED partial_tp_fast: ws_timeout_after_send — the SAME failure mode as SUIUSDT. This occurred 35 minutes AFTER the runner was restarted with the H1 audit fix. The ambiguous handler at grid_bot_service.py line 20098 should have caught this but did NOT. Bot is now in error state with orphaned PUMPFUNUSDT position.
evidence: runner.log 09:45:38: CLOSE_FAILED partial_tp_fast ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 09:45:52: Bot is in ERROR state: None
bybit_client.py H1 fix at line 348: returns ambiguous=True for ws_timeout_after_send
grid_bot_service.py line 20098: elif _is_ambiguous_order_result(close_result)
grid_bot_service.py line 20111: else: _hard_fail_close reached DESPITE ambiguous handler
Runner started at 09:10 with new code — .pyc timestamps confirm new code loaded
suspected_root_cause: UNKNOWN — the code flow analysis shows the ambiguous handler SHOULD catch ws_timeout_after_send, but evidence proves it does not. Possible causes: (1) a different create_order path that bypasses the H1 fix, (2) the order router transforming the result, (3) a Python import/caching issue not detected by .pyc timestamps. THE H1 FIX HAS A COVERAGE GAP.
notes_for_fixer: CRITICAL: The H1 audit fix does NOT fully prevent ws_timeout_after_send from triggering _hard_fail_close. Two bots have now been orphaned by this failure mode (SUIUSDT and PUMPFUNUSDT). The partial_tp_fast code path at line 20039 calls _create_order_checked, which calls self.client.create_order (bybit_client), which should return ambiguous=True. But _hard_fail_close was reached anyway. Need to add defensive logging INSIDE _is_ambiguous_order_result to trace what close_result actually contains when this path is reached.

summary: Bot storage cache_lock timeout 64 times in 7 minutes since restart (~9/min)
symptom: Runner log shows 'Bot storage cache_lock timed out after 0.250s' approximately 9 times per minute since the 09:50:42 restart. Lock contention between runner tick loop and dashboard/snapshot processes accessing bots.json simultaneously.
evidence: runner.log: 64 cache_lock timeout warnings in 7 minutes (09:50-09:57)
Pattern: timeouts correlate with error state check cycles
First timeout at 09:55:00, then 09:56:24, 09:57:30
suspected_root_cause: The runner's fast tick loop (1s interval) and the dashboard's snapshot generation both read bots.json via bot_storage.list_bots(). The fcntl.flock with 0.250s timeout causes lock contention. With error-state bots, the runner still iterates all bots every tick even though none are running.
notes_for_fixer: Check bot_storage_service.py cache_lock timeout value and read pattern. Consider: (a) longer timeout, (b) read-only cache for list_bots, (c) skip full bot iteration when no running bots. Current rate of 9/min is high even for idle state.

summary: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: Margin monitor skips on 'Amount below minimum' with no retry or fallback
symptom: Margin monitor detected low liq distance but exchange rejected the add amount
evidence: margin_monitor_service.py: After 'Amount below minimum', code does `continue` (line 446) — no retry with larger amount
No fallback action (partial close) exists in the service
Amount calculation: available_balance * ratio * severity — when this is too small, nothing happens
Cooldown skipped for first-run and critical, but the failure handler itself just skips
suspected_root_cause: margin_monitor_service.py line 444-446: when exchange returns 'Amount below minimum', the service records the error and skips the position with `continue`. No retry with a larger amount meeting Bybit's minimum, no fallback to partial close, no escalation. The calculated amount ($0.10) was below Bybit's minimum transfer.
notes_for_fixer: When 'Amount below minimum' is returned: (a) retry with Bybit's minimum margin transfer amount ($1.00 or similar), OR (b) trigger partial position close to reduce exposure, OR (c) escalate with a louder warning. Currently the service silently gives up.

summary: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: CLOSE_FAILED is permanent error with zero auto-recovery. FIX-001 prevents entry but recovery path still missing.
symptom: Bot 8580f06d entered error state at 09:14 and has no path back to running
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:3476-3490: _hard_fail_close() sets status=error permanently
runner.py:109: FULL_CYCLE_ELIGIBLE_STATUSES = {running, paused, recovering, stop_cleanup_pending} — error NOT included
runner.py:1034-1045: error bots logged then `continue` (skipped from all cycle processing)
guardian_service.py:424-431: _check_error_persist only logs, returns 0 heals, doesn't check positions
Only auto-recovery: 'below minimum' grid count adjustment — not applicable to CLOSE_FAILED
FIX-001 prevents bots from entering error on ws_timeout (returns ambiguous instead) — validated with 3 events on TAOUSDT
suspected_root_cause: CLOSE_FAILED is intentionally terminal — prevents blind re-entry. But there's no exchange verification step: if the close actually succeeded (order went through despite timeout), the bot should be able to verify and recover. Currently requires manual intervention.
notes_for_fixer: FIX-001 prevents the entry into error state (validated in production). But if a bot does end up in error with an open position, there should be a recovery path: (1) verify exchange state, (2) if position is closed on exchange, clear error and transition to stopped, (3) if position still open, offer manual recovery via dashboard. Currently, error bots need manual status reset.

summary: AMBIGUOUS ESCALATION CRITICAL: unresolved ambiguous close outcomes accumulating for 30-50 min
symptom: PUMPFUNUSDT has pending ambiguous outcomes from 09:45 (3076s=51min). TAOUSDT has pending from ~10:06 (1784s=30min). These are being escalated as CRITICAL but not resolved by reconciliation.
evidence: runner.log 10:35:38: PUMPFUNUSDT AMBIGUOUS ESCALATION CRITICAL: pending 2999s
runner.log 10:36:50: TAOUSDT AMBIGUOUS ESCALATION CRITICAL: pending 1784s
runner.log 10:36:53: PUMPFUNUSDT AMBIGUOUS ESCALATION CRITICAL: pending 3076s
suspected_root_cause: When ws_timeout_after_send occurs, the outcome is marked as 'pending ambiguous'. Exchange reconciliation should verify whether the order was executed and clear the pending marker. The reconciliation is either not checking these markers, or the markers persist even after positions are partially closed via other orders.
notes_for_fixer: Check grid_bot_service.py for how ambiguous pending markers are tracked and how reconciliation clears them. The escalation to CRITICAL after extended pending is correct behavior, but the underlying issue is that reconciliation never resolves them. Also check if PUMPFUNUSDT markers persist because the bot is in error state (no cycle to run reconciliation).

summary: Error-state bot shows 6 open orders in dashboard but exchange has 0 — stale internal order counters never reconciled
symptom: PUMPFUNUSDT bot 6055cb3b displays open_order_count=6 (5 entry + 1 exit) and exchange_open_orders_detected=True in the dashboard, but the exchange snapshot shows 0 PUMPFUNUSDT open orders. The bot entered error state at 09:45 and its internal counters were never updated because error bots don't cycle. Operator sees false order counts.
evidence: bots.json PUMPFUNUSDT: open_order_count=6, entry_orders_open=5, exit_orders_open=1, exchange_open_orders_detected=True
runtime_snapshot_bridge.json open_orders: symbols={TAOUSDT} — NO PUMPFUNUSDT orders on exchange
ambiguous_execution_follow_up: status=still_unresolved, pending 65+ min
Bot status=error since 09:45, cycle never runs to reconcile counters
suspected_root_cause: When a bot enters error state: (1) runner auto-cancels exchange orders via _cancel_orders_on_error(), (2) but the bot's internal order counters (open_order_count, entry_orders_open, exit_orders_open, exchange_open_orders_detected) are NOT zeroed, (3) error-state bots skip the full cycle so reconciliation never corrects them, (4) dashboard reads stale internal counters and displays phantom orders.
notes_for_fixer: When a bot enters error state (or when _cancel_orders_on_error runs): zero out open_order_count, entry_orders_open, exit_orders_open, and set exchange_open_orders_detected=False. Also: the ambiguous_execution_follow_up should be resolved during error-state reconciliation (runner.py:990-1015) even if the bot isn't cycling.

summary: Running bot paralyzed: ambiguous flag blocks ALL order placement for 47+ min, position has zero protection
symptom: TAOUSDT bot is status=running with a position (0.013 qty, $3.73) but has 0 open orders on exchange. Grid logic detects 6 levels needed, tries to place 3 buys + 3 sells, but places 0: sells skipped (reduce_only_exhausted), buys blocked by _block_opening_orders set by unresolved ambiguous close outcome. Bot has been unable to place any orders for 47+ minutes.
evidence: runner.log 10:54:17: Profit lock close AMBIGUOUS ws_timeout_after_send
runner.log 10:54:18: '6 levels need orders (current: 0, buys: 3, sells: 3)'
runner.log 10:54:18: 'Placed 0 new orders, 0 failed'
runner.log 10:54:18: 'Skips: [reduce_only_exhausted:3]'
runner.log 10:54:21: AMBIGUOUS ESCALATION CRITICAL: pending 2836s
Bot open_order_count=0, exchange orders=0, position=0.013 qty
suspected_root_cause: The ambiguous close handler correctly sets _block_opening_orders to prevent new orders during ambiguous state. But the ambiguous follow-up reconciliation never resolves the pending outcome (pending 47+ min). The result: the bot is running but permanently blocked from placing ANY orders. The position has no TP, no grid, no protection. This is a NEW failure mode — the bot doesn't crash to error state (FIX-001 working) but becomes functionally paralyzed.
notes_for_fixer: CRITICAL: The bot appears running but is paralyzed. The ambiguous follow-up must resolve within a bounded time. Options: (1) after N minutes of unresolved ambiguous, force exchange reconciliation to verify order state and clear the flag, (2) add a timeout to _block_opening_orders that auto-clears after exchange verification, (3) the ESCALATION CRITICAL already forces recheck but the recheck doesn't clear the flag — fix the recheck logic.

summary: SECOND status filter gap: ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES in bot_status_service.py:41 also excludes error — open orders for error bots invisible
symptom: PUMPFUNUSDT bot (error state) shows 6 orders in dashboard internal counters but exchange open_orders query returns 0 for PUMPFUNUSDT. ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES in bot_status_service.py:41 is used for open order queries and also lacks error/risk_stopped/stop_cleanup_pending. This means: (1) open orders for error-state bots are never queried from exchange, (2) the system doesn't know if those orders still exist on Bybit, (3) potential orphaned orders on exchange burning margin.
notes_for_fixer: Add error, risk_stopped, stop_cleanup_pending to ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES at bot_status_service.py:41. This is the SAME fix as FIX-002 but for a different file. Also: grep for ALL status filter sets across the codebase that exclude error — there may be more.

suspected_root_cause: Bybit WebSocket connection is unstable — ping/pong timeouts every 3-5 minutes on average. notes_for_fixer: Investigate bybit_stream_service.py: (1) check if there is a proactive reconnect strategy (reconnect every N hours before ping/pong timeout), (2) check if ping interval is too long, (3) check if the WS keepalive matches Bybit's server-side timeout. The current approach is reactive (reconnect on drop) — a proactive reconnect before timeout may improve reliability.

However: (1) Recovery path for existing error-state bots still missing. (2) MON-014 shows _block_opening_orders set by FIX-001 ambiguous handler is NEVER cleared when position exists, causing infinite bot paralysis. The ambiguous follow-up resolution at grid_bot_service.py:6976-6978 does NOT clear _block_opening_orders. The _clear_opening_block_flags at line 9526 does NOT include _block_opening_orders. Fix is a net improvement over old behavior (running+paralyzed beats error+orphaned) but needs bounded resolution.
approved_scope: FIX-001 ambiguous close handling is approved. The _block_opening_orders clearance gap needs a follow-up fix.
additional_validation_required: MON-014 fix: bounded timeout on _block_opening_orders after exchange state verification. When ambiguous follow-up resolves or expires, _block_opening_orders must be cleared.
reviewer_summary: FIX-001 is production-proven (117+ AMBIGUOUS survived). But it introduces MON-014: the _block_opening_orders flag set by the ambiguous handler has NO clearance path when the position still exists. The bot stays 'running' but places zero orders indefinitely. This is strictly better than the old error+orphaned behavior, but needs a bounded resolution fix.

summary: GATE ASSESSMENT: _block_opening_orders has NO bounded clearance after ambiguous follow-up — confirmed via code analysis
symptom: Running bot paralyzed: places 0 orders for 47+ min, position unprotected
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:6976-6978: ambiguous follow-up resolution does NOT clear _block_opening_orders
grid_bot_service.py:9526-9549: _clear_opening_block_flags does NOT include _block_opening_orders in its clearing set
grid_bot_service.py:12846-12848: cycle-start clearance only fires if _upnl_stoploss_reason is also set (not set by ambiguous handler)
grid_bot_service.py:13182-13208: no-position clearance only fires if position is gone (position exists in MON-014)
All 6 ambiguous handlers (lines 20353, 20526, 21055, 21829, 23215, 24139) set _block_opening_orders=True with NO corresponding clearance
ESCALATION CRITICAL fires every ~60s but recheck at line 6949-6963 only escalates — never clears the flag
suspected_root_cause: The ambiguous close handler correctly blocks opening orders during ambiguity. But NO code path clears _block_opening_orders when: (1) the ambiguous follow-up resolves, (2) the marker expires, or (3) the escalation reaches CRITICAL. The only clearance is when the position no longer exists — which never happens if the close was ambiguous and the position survived.
notes_for_fixer: CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE via code tracing. Fix needed at grid_bot_service.py:6976-6978 (ambiguous follow-up resolution): when marker.pending becomes False, also clear bot._block_opening_orders. Also add bounded timeout: if _block_opening_orders has been True for >300s AND the ambiguous follow-up is resolved/expired, force clear. Risk Class C — needs careful testing. The fix must NOT clear _block_opening_orders if a genuinely unresolved ambiguous state exists.
promotion_gate_reason: Gate assessment only — no fix has been applied. Root cause confirmed via code analysis. This is a Class C execution issue requiring a bounded timeout fix. The fix is straightforward (clear _block_opening_orders when ambiguous follow-up resolves) but touches execution control flow. Needs tests + paper evidence.
additional_validation_required: Fix implementation, tests, paper/shadow evidence of bounded clearance working correctly
reviewer_summary: Definitive root cause: 6 ambiguous handlers set _block_opening_orders=True. Zero code paths clear it when follow-up resolves/expires and position still exists. Follow-up fix to FIX-001.

notes_for_fixer: Fix: after cancel_all_orders in _cancel_orders_on_error, also zero out bot[open_order_count], bot[entry_orders_open], bot[exit_orders_open], bot[exchange_open_orders_detected]=False, then save_bot. Risk Class A (display truthfulness, no trading logic).

reviewer_summary: Confirmed: _cancel_orders_on_error cancels exchange orders but doesn't update internal bot counters. Class A fix (display), low risk.

summary: Running bot paralyzed by unresolved ambiguous flag — FIXED
symptom: TAOUSDT bot: 0 orders for 47+ min due to _block_opening_orders never clearing after ambiguous close
confirmed_root_cause: Three-part bug: (1) _classify_ambiguous_execution_follow_up returned still_unresolved/pending=True for reduce_only actions when position exists, even after market close orders are gone from exchange. (2) reconcile_bots_exchange_truth never cleared _block_opening_orders when marker resolved. (3) _get_exchange_truth_opening_blocker blocked on truth_check_expired and still_unresolved even when pending=False (post-expiry), causing permanent paralysis.
regression_risk: CLASS C — touches execution path. Change 1: reduce_only marker now resolves after 10s if no open order visible (correct for market close). Change 2: _block_opening_orders cleared on resolution (no other clear path existed). Change 3: pending=False markers no longer block (correct — non-pending means outcome determined). Risk: a slow/limit close order that IS open would be caught by earlier order_link_id check, not affected.
exact_ui_location_if_any: Bot card > Order count badge (0 orders while running)
fixer_summary: Three surgical changes to grid_bot_service.py. (1) _classify_ambiguous_execution_follow_up: reduce_only branch now returns no_pending_close_order/pending=False when order not visible in open orders — correct since market closes execute immediately. (2) reconcile_bots_exchange_truth: clears _block_opening_orders when marker resolves if not held by UPnL SL. (3) _get_exchange_truth_opening_blocker: condition simplified to only check pending=True — expired/non-pending markers must not block indefinitely. CLASS C: requires gate review and replay/shadow evidence before production.

summary: ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES in bot_status_service.py excludes error — open order query blind to error-state bots — FIXED
symptom: Error-state bots' symbols not included in live open orders query — dashboard may show stale order counts
confirmed_root_cause: bot_status_service.py:41 ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES excludes error/risk_stopped/stop_cleanup_pending. Line 3750 uses this set to build the symbols list for exchange open order queries. Same gap as MON-001 but in a different file/constant.
focused_test_results: All 1057 tests pass. No bot_status_service-specific tests for this path.
regression_risk: LOW — added new constant LIVE_ORDER_OWNER_STATUSES, used only in _build_live_open_orders_by_symbol. ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES unchanged (preserves readiness, latency path, preview semantics). Only the open-order query now includes error/risk_stopped/stop_cleanup_pending symbols.
exact_ui_location_if_any: Bot card > Open Orders count (was stale for error-state bots)
fixer_summary: Added LIVE_ORDER_OWNER_STATUSES = ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES | {error, risk_stopped, stop_cleanup_pending} at line 47. Changed _build_live_open_orders_by_symbol to use LIVE_ORDER_OWNER_STATUSES for symbol collection. ACTIVE_POSITION_OWNER_STATUSES left unchanged to avoid readiness/display side-effects. CLASS B: dashboard truthfulness fix.

summary: Error-state bot shows stale order counters — FIXED
symptom: PUMPFUNUSDT bot shows open_order_count=6 while exchange has 0. Dashboard misleads operator.
confirmed_root_cause: _cancel_orders_on_error in runner.py cancels exchange orders but never zeros bot.open_order_count, entry_orders_open, exit_orders_open. reconcile_bots_exchange_truth updates exchange_open_orders_detected but not these internal counters. Result: stale non-zero counter survives restart.
focused_test_results: 1057 passed. No dedicated test for _cancel_orders_on_error counter zeroing.
regression_risk: LOW — zeroing is done on the bot dict in-place before reconciliation saves it. reconcile_bots_exchange_truth copies the bot via dict(bot) so changes flow through. Counter zeroing is unconditional but safe: reconciliation will re-confirm exchange state. Only applies when bot enters error state.
exact_ui_location_if_any: Bot card > Open Orders badge (was showing phantom order counts)
fixer_summary: Added 3-line zero-reset for open_order_count/entry_orders_open/exit_orders_open at end of _cancel_orders_on_error. Runs regardless of whether exchange cancel succeeded. Reconciliation that follows will confirm actual state. CLASS A/B: display truthfulness and state integrity.

summary: UNIUSDT bot 5af10f53 ambiguous reduce-only SELL order unresolved — position unclosed
symptom: Bot issued CORRELATED EXIT + SMART EXIT sell orders at 16:20:12 UTC, both returned ws_timeout AMBIGUOUS. Position 12.4 UNIUSDT long remains open with pending=True ambiguous_execution_follow_up. Guardian is logging ambiguous_escalation every ~60s since 15:12 UTC (now 400+ seconds).
evidence: runner.log 16:20:12 UTC: '[UNIUSDT] WebSocket order AMBIGUOUS (timeout after send) — NOT falling back to REST to prevent duplicates'; runner.log 16:20:15: second AMBIGUOUS; bots.json 5af10f53 ambiguous_follow_up status=pending,pending=True,order_link_id=gen_5af10f53_412800Sc,created_at=2026-03-16T15:12:55Z,expires_at=2026-03-16T15:35:15Z; runner.log 16:20:09: CORRELATED EXIT closing to protect profit /bin/bash.08; guardian_healing_log: ambiguous_escalation pending 404s at 15:19:39Z
suspected_root_cause: WebSocket send timeout on reduce-only close orders — outcome unknown, position may or may not be partially closed. FIX-006 pending=true branch leaves this correctly unresolved but the position is at risk if actual close did not execute.
notes_for_fixer: Position 12.4 UNIUSDT long at entry ~4.105, mark ~4.113, pct_to_liq=15.1%. Two close orders hit ws_timeout. Operator should verify exchange state and manually close if needed. Code path: grid_bot_service.py CORRELATED EXIT + SMART EXIT both hitting ws_timeout on the same sell. expires_at is 15:35:15Z — check if FIX-006 auto-resolution fired or will fire at expiry.

summary: Private WebSocket position/order event topics silent for 496+ seconds — WS event stream stale
symptom: Private WS topics 'position' and 'order' last received an event at timestamp ~443s ago (15:11:28Z) as of 15:20:04Z observation. Execution topic 488s ago. Cache is marked fresh=True via REST fallback but live WS events are not flowing. priv_reconnect_epoch=2.
evidence: runtime_snapshot_bridge.json meta.stream_health.topic_last_message_at: position=1773673956.32s (age 443s at obs time), order=1773673956.32s (age 443s), execution=1773673910.96s (age 488s); private_cache.position.fresh=True (REST-sourced); private_cache.order.fresh=True (REST-sourced); max_age_sec=5.0 exceeded severely by WS event gap
suspected_root_cause: Private WebSocket connection appears to have stopped delivering events for position/order topics despite being authenticated (priv_reconnect_epoch=2 suggests one reconnect occurred). This is the same pattern as MON-002/MON-017 — WS reliability issue.
notes_for_fixer: REST cache is compensating (fresh=True via rest_fetch) so data is not entirely stale, but WS event latency means execution events may arrive late or not at all. The ambiguous order at 16:20:12 UTC may be related — if position events are not flowing, the ambiguous resolution check cannot confirm via WS.
